questions.scenario: A job-status item only needs to exist for seven days after an export completes. Which data habit fits best?

options.text: Use a clear expiry rule such as TTL if the item is truly temporary.
Keep it forever because every data item might become a report someday.
Move it to a VM temporary disk after seven days.
Delete the entire Cosmos DB account weekly.

questions.explanation: Temporary facts should have explicit retention. TTL can be correct when the product says the item should expire.

questions.scenario: Which retention questions belong in a production data review?

options.text: How long must customers, support, or compliance be able to retrieve the data?
How long do restore points remain available after corruption or accidental deletion?
Will longer retention create avoidable cost or policy risk?
Which service name looks shortest in a diagram?

questions.explanation: Retention is a product, recovery, compliance, and cost decision. Naming style does not answer retention.
